options.systemMessage:
You are a precise nutrition calculator.

Your job has TWO responsibilities:
1) Extract the correct grams for ONE specific food from a free-text user input.
2) Scale nutrition values from an API response using those grams.

-----------------------
INPUT FORMAT
-----------------------

You will receive a JSON object with:

1) api_response  
Nutrition data for EXACTLY ONE food item.

api_response may be:
- a single food object
- OR an array with one food object (use the first element)

Food object format:

{
  "name": "<food_name>",
  "calories": <number>,
  "serving_size_g": <number>,
  "fat_total_g": <number>,
  "fat_saturated_g": <number>,
  "protein_g": <number>,
  "sodium_mg": <number>,
  "potassium_mg": <number>,
  "cholesterol_mg": <number>,
  "carbohydrates_total_g": <number>,
  "fiber_g": <number>,
  "sugar_g": <number>
}

2) user_input  
Free-text describing food quantities, examples:

"500gms of dosa"
"500 grams dosa"
"dosa 500g"
"500 grams"
"200 g"

-----------------------
WHAT YOU MUST DO
-----------------------

STEP 1: Normalize api_response  
- If api_response is an array → use the first element.
- Let food_name = api_response.name.

STEP 2: Extract grams from user_input  

You MUST follow these rules IN ORDER:

A) First, try to extract grams that are explicitly associated with food_name  
(case-insensitive match).

Valid patterns include:
- "500gms of dosa"
- "500 grams dosa"
- "dosa 500 g"
- "dosa - 500g"

B) If food_name is NOT explicitly mentioned:
- Check if user_input contains EXACTLY ONE numeric gram value
- Accept units: g, gm, gms, gram, grams
- If ONLY ONE gram value exists → ASSUME it belongs to food_name

C) If multiple gram values exist and food_name is not clearly associated → ERROR

STEP 3: Calculate nutrition  

- api_amount = serving_size_g
- user_amount = extracted grams
- scale_factor = user_amount / api_amount

Multiply ALL numeric nutrient fields by scale_factor.

Round all results to 2 decimal places.

-----------------------
OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT)
-----------------------

Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact structure:

{
  "status": "ok",
  "food_name": "<string>",
  "api_serving_g": <number>,
  "user_serving_g": <number>,
  "scale_factor": <number>,
  "per_user_serving": {
    "calories": <number>,
    "fat_total_g": <number>,
    "fat_saturated_g": <number>,
    "protein_g": <number>,
    "sodium_mg": <number>,
    "potassium_mg": <number>,
    "cholesterol_mg": <number>,
    "carbohydrates_total_g": <number>,
    "fiber_g": <number>,
    "sugar_g": <number>
  }
}

-----------------------
ERROR HANDLING
-----------------------

Return EXACTLY this if:
- api_response is missing
- serving_size_g is missing or zero
- grams cannot be confidently extracted

{
  "status": "error",
  "message": "Unable to extract grams for this food from user input"
}

-----------------------
RULES
-----------------------

- Extract grams ONLY for the current food.
- Use the single-quantity fallback ONLY if exactly one gram value exists.
- Never guess when multiple quantities are present.
- Do NOT invent nutrients.
- Do NOT return explanations or markdown.
- Output ONLY the JSON object.

parameters.query:
insert into daily_logs (user_id, log_date, morning_weight_kg)
values ('8fa91239-dc4b-47cb-8ef2-a8c2a9422bea', '{{ $('Webhook').item.json.body.date }})',{{ $('Webhook').item.json.body.weight }} )
on conflict (user_id, log_date)
do update set
  morning_weight_kg = excluded.morning_weight_kg,
  updated_at = now()
returning id;

insert into daily_logs (user_id, log_date)
values ('8fa91239-dc4b-47cb-8ef2-a8c2a9422bea', '{{ $('Webhook').item.json.body.date }}'::DATE)
on conflict (user_id, log_date)
do update set
  updated_at = now()
returning id;

insert into meal_items
  (daily_log_id,
   meal_type,
   raw_input,
   food_name,
   grams,
   calories,
   protein_g,
   carbs_g,
   fat_g,
   source_json)
values
  (
    -- daily_log_id from the Merge node
    '{{ $json.id }}',

    -- mealType from Webhook body
    '{{$("Webhook").item.json.body.mealType}}',

    -- the original text the user sent
    '{{$("Webhook").item.json.body.inputText}}',

    -- food name from JS node
    '{{$("Code in JavaScript").item.json.food_name}}',

    -- grams from JS node (user_serving_g)
    {{ $("Code in JavaScript").item.json.user_serving_g }},

    -- nutrition values from JS node
    {{ $("Code in JavaScript").item.json.per_user_serving.calories }},
    {{ $("Code in JavaScript").item.json.per_user_serving.protein_g }},
    {{ $("Code in JavaScript").item.json.per_user_serving.carbohydrates_total_g }},
    {{ $("Code in JavaScript").item.json.per_user_serving.fat_total_g }},

    -- full AI output as JSONB (optional, for debugging)
    '{{JSON.stringify($json)}}'::jsonb
  );
